3.6 Compute the actual conversion rate of top 10 percentile Mx leads

actual_icp_conversion <- mean(top_10_mx$progressed == "yes")
actual_icp_conversion
## [1] 0.752451
uplift <- actual_icp_conversion / baseline_mx_conversion
uplift
## [1] 5.804825
  1. Optional: Visualize the uplift
library(ggplot2)

df <- tibble(
  segment = c("Baseline Mx", "Top 10% ICP Mx"),
  conversion = c(baseline_mx_conversion, actual_icp_conversion)
)

ggplot(df, aes(x = segment, y = conversion, fill = segment)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title = "Conversion Rate: Baseline Mx vs ICP Top 10%",
    y = "Conversion Rate",
    x = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

## The codes and the plot for conversion rate vs every percentile of Mx in the dataset.

3.7 Step 1 — Filter Leads to Mx and score

mx_scored <- predict(final_fit, mx_data, type = "prob") %>%
  bind_cols(mx_data) %>%
  arrange(desc(.pred_yes)) %>%
  mutate(rank = row_number())
mx_scored
## # A tibble: 4,081 × 29
##    .pred_yes .pred_no acct_primary_site_function   acct_manufacturing_model     
##        <dbl>    <dbl> <chr>                        <chr>                        
##  1     0.767    0.233 Diagnostic Equipment         Original Equipment Manufactu…
##  2     0.745    0.255 Wearable health monitors     In-House                     
##  3     0.743    0.257 Sterile injectables          CDMO                         
##  4     0.739    0.261 Tissue products              CMO                          
##  5     0.727    0.273 Nutraceutical manufacturing  CDMO                         
##  6     0.724    0.276 Medical cannabis formulation In-House                     
##  7     0.717    0.283 Sterile injectables          CMO                          
##  8     0.713    0.287 Not Enough Info Found        In-House                     
##  9     0.711    0.289 Endoscope reprocessing       In-House                     
## 10     0.701    0.299 Not Enough Info Found        503A                         
## # ℹ 4,071 more rows
## # ℹ 25 more variables: acct_target_industry <chr>, contact_lead_title <chr>,
## #   qal_id <chr>, contact_lead_id <chr>, next_stage_c <chr>, priority <chr>,
## #   acct_territory_rollup <chr>, acct_tier_rollup <chr>, solution <chr>,
## #   solution_rollup <chr>, last_tactic_campaign_channel <chr>,
## #   qal_cohort_date <date>, title_missing <chr>, progressed <fct>,
## #   cohort_year <dbl>, cohort_month <dbl>, cohort_day <int>, …

3.8 Step 2: Compute conversion rate at every percentile

library(dplyr)
library(purrr)

percentiles <- seq(0.01, 1, by = 0.01)

mx_curve <- map_df(percentiles, function(p) {
  cutoff <- floor(nrow(mx_scored) * p)
  
  tibble(
    percentile = p * 100,
    conversion_rate = mean(mx_scored$progressed[1:cutoff] == "yes")
  )
})

mx_curve
## # A tibble: 100 × 2
##    percentile conversion_rate
##         <dbl>           <dbl>
##  1          1           0.95 
##  2          2           0.951
##  3          3           0.918
##  4          4           0.896
##  5          5           0.868
##  6          6           0.848
##  7          7           0.814
##  8          8           0.791
##  9          9           0.771
## 10         10           0.752
## # ℹ 90 more rows
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)

percentiles <- seq(0.01, 1, by = 0.01)

mx_curve_detailed <- map_df(percentiles, function(p) {
  cutoff <- floor(nrow(mx_scored) * p)

  mx_scored_slice <- mx_scored[1:cutoff, ]

  mx_scored_slice %>%
    mutate(
      percentile = p * 100,
      conversion_rate = mean(mx_scored_slice$progressed == "yes"),
      mx_prob = .pred_yes        # <-- Mx probability score for each row
    ) %>%
    select(
      percentile,
      conversion_rate,
      progressed,
      .pred_yes,
      .pred_no,
      mx_prob
    )
})

mx_curve_detailed
## # A tibble: 206,041 × 6
##    percentile conversion_rate progressed .pred_yes .pred_no mx_prob
##         <dbl>           <dbl> <fct>          <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>
##  1          1            0.95 yes            0.767    0.233   0.767
##  2          1            0.95 yes            0.745    0.255   0.745
##  3          1            0.95 yes            0.743    0.257   0.743
##  4          1            0.95 yes            0.739    0.261   0.739
##  5          1            0.95 yes            0.727    0.273   0.727
##  6          1            0.95 yes            0.724    0.276   0.724
##  7          1            0.95 yes            0.717    0.283   0.717
##  8          1            0.95 yes            0.713    0.287   0.713
##  9          1            0.95 yes            0.711    0.289   0.711
## 10          1            0.95 yes            0.701    0.299   0.701
## # ℹ 206,031 more rows

build a detailed mx_curve that includes counts

library(dplyr)
library(purrr)

percentiles <- seq(0.01, 1, by = 0.01)

mx_curve <- map_df(percentiles, function(p) {
  cutoff <- floor(nrow(mx_scored) * p)

  mx_slice <- mx_scored[1:cutoff, ]

  tibble(
    percentile = p * 100,
    n_total = cutoff,
    n_converted = sum(mx_slice$progressed == "yes"),
    conversion_rate = n_converted / n_total
  )
})

Objective: maximize total conversions

mx_curve <- mx_curve %>%
  arrange(percentile) %>%
  mutate(
    incremental_converted = n_converted - lag(n_converted, default = 0),
    cum_conversions = cumsum(incremental_converted)
  )
mx_curve %>%
  mutate(cum_conversions = cumsum(n_converted))
## # A tibble: 100 × 6
##    percentile n_total n_converted conversion_rate incremental_converted
##         <dbl>   <dbl>       <int>           <dbl>                 <int>
##  1          1      40          38           0.95                     38
##  2          2      81          77           0.951                    39
##  3          3     122         112           0.918                    35
##  4          4     163         146           0.896                    34
##  5          5     204         177           0.868                    31
##  6          6     244         207           0.848                    30
##  7          7     285         232           0.814                    25
##  8          8     326         258           0.791                    26
##  9          9     367         283           0.771                    25
## 10         10     408         307           0.752                    24
## # ℹ 90 more rows
## # ℹ 1 more variable: cum_conversions <int>

3.9 Plot of Conversion Rate vs Percentile of Mx Leads

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(mx_curve, aes(x = percentile, y = conversion_rate)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", size = 1.2) +
  labs(
    title = "Conversion Rate vs Percentile of Mx Leads",
    x = "% of Leads (ranked by model score)",
    y = "Conversion Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

## Plot of Cumulative Conversion Rate vs Percentile of Mx Leads

ggplot(mx_curve, aes(x = percentile, y = cum_conversions)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkred", size = 1.2) +
  labs(
    title = "Cumulative Conversions vs Percentile",
    x = "% of Leads (ranked by model score)",
    y = "Cumulative Conversions"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

3.10 Cumulative conversion table for 30th percentile

mx_curve_early <- mx_curve %>%
  filter(percentile <= 30) %>%
  select(percentile, n_total, n_converted, incremental_converted, cum_conversions)

mx_curve_early
## # A tibble: 30 × 5
##    percentile n_total n_converted incremental_converted cum_conversions
##         <dbl>   <dbl>       <int>                 <int>           <int>
##  1          1      40          38                    38              38
##  2          2      81          77                    39              77
##  3          3     122         112                    35             112
##  4          4     163         146                    34             146
##  5          5     204         177                    31             177
##  6          6     244         207                    30             207
##  7          7     285         232                    25             232
##  8          8     326         258                    26             258
##  9          9     367         283                    25             283
## 10         10     408         307                    24             307
## # ℹ 20 more rows

3.11 Cumulative conversion table for 100th percentile

mx_curve_detailed %>% 
  filter(percentile == 100)
## # A tibble: 4,081 × 6
##    percentile conversion_rate progressed .pred_yes .pred_no mx_prob
##         <dbl>           <dbl> <fct>          <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>
##  1        100           0.130 yes            0.767    0.233   0.767
##  2        100           0.130 yes            0.745    0.255   0.745
##  3        100           0.130 yes            0.743    0.257   0.743
##  4        100           0.130 yes            0.739    0.261   0.739
##  5        100           0.130 yes            0.727    0.273   0.727
##  6        100           0.130 yes            0.724    0.276   0.724
##  7        100           0.130 yes            0.717    0.283   0.717
##  8        100           0.130 yes            0.713    0.287   0.713
##  9        100           0.130 yes            0.711    0.289   0.711
## 10        100           0.130 yes            0.701    0.299   0.701
## # ℹ 4,071 more rows

3.12 drop in the conversion rate and get the corresponding percentile

drop_point <- mx_curve %>%
  filter(conversion_rate < .46) %>%
  slice(1)
drop_point
## # A tibble: 1 × 6
##   percentile n_total n_converted conversion_rate incremental_converted
##        <dbl>   <dbl>       <int>           <dbl>                 <int>
## 1         25    1020         468           0.459                     8
## # ℹ 1 more variable: cum_conversions <int>

4 1-25 in the increments of percentiles and their conversion rates

target_percentiles <- c(1, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25)

mx_curve %>%
  filter(percentile %in% target_percentiles) %>%
  mutate(conversion_rate = round(conversion_rate, 2)) %>%
  select(percentile, conversion_rate) %>%
  arrange(percentile)
## # A tibble: 5 × 2
##   percentile conversion_rate
##        <dbl>           <dbl>
## 1          1            0.95
## 2          5            0.87
## 3         10            0.75
## 4         20            0.53
## 5         25            0.46

4.1 An ideal high lead

high_lead1 <- tibble(acct_primary_site_function = "Diagnostic Equipment",
  acct_manufacturing_model = "Original Equipment Manufacturing",
  acct_target_industry = "Medical Device",
  contact_lead_title = "Quality Compliance Project Manager",
  qal_id = "a0dPQ000003EVotYAG",
  contact_lead_id = "003PQ00000lZoJtYAK",
  next_stage_c = "SQL",
  priority = "P1 - Contact Us",
  acct_territory_rollup = "EMEA",
  acct_tier_rollup = "Small",
  solution = "Manufacturing",
  solution_rollup = "Mx",
  last_tactic_campaign_channel = "Direct/Inbound",
  qal_cohort_date = as.Date("2025-02-06"),
  title_missing = "Not Missing",
  progressed = factor("yes", levels = "yes"),
  cohort_year = 2025,
  cohort_month = 2,
  cohort_day = 6,
  cohort_wday                  = "Tue",
  cohort_week = 6,
  cohort_quarter = 1,
  cohort_age_days = 416,
  manu_group = factor("Manufacturing"),
  title_clean = "Quality Compliance Project Manager",
  title_group = factor("Manager"),
  rank_overall = 76,
  top_10_flag = 1
)

4.2 Scoring a high lead

predict(final_fit, high_lead1, type = "prob")
#predict(final_fit, low_lead, type = "prob")

4.3 Typical Low lead features

lead_true_low <- tibble(
  acct_primary_site_function = "Corporate Services",
  acct_manufacturing_model = "Outsourced",
  acct_target_industry = "Retail",
  contact_lead_title = "Administrative Assistant",
  qal_id = "a0dTEST000000999",
  contact_lead_id = "003TEST000000999",
  next_stage_c = "MQL",
  priority = "P4 - Low",
  acct_territory_rollup = "APAC",
  acct_tier_rollup = "Small",
  solution = "Manufacturing",
  solution_rollup = "Mx",
  last_tactic_campaign_channel = "Paid Social",
  qal_cohort_date = as.Date("2023-11-15"),
  title_missing = "Not Missing",
  cohort_year = 2023,
  cohort_month = 11,
  cohort_day = 15,
  cohort_wday   = "Mon",
  cohort_week = 46,
  cohort_quarter = 4,
  cohort_age_days = 870,
  manu_group = factor("Non-Manufacturing"),
  title_clean = "Administrative Assistant",
  title_group = factor("Support"),
  rank_overall = 450,
  top_10_flag = 0
)

4.4 Scoring low lead

 predict(final_fit, lead_true_low, type = "prob")
## Warning: Novel level found in column "title_group": "Support".
## ℹ The level has been removed, and values have been coerced to <NA>.
## # A tibble: 1 × 2
##   .pred_yes .pred_no
##       <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1     0.380    0.620

4.5 Extract the ICP segment (top 20% of Mx leads)

mx_icp.2 <- mx_scored %>%
  slice(1:floor(0.2 * n()))

4.6 Summarize the ICP for the top 20 percentile for Industry profile

mx_icp.2 %>%
  count(acct_target_industry, sort = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n))
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##   acct_target_industry     n     pct
##   <chr>                <int>   <dbl>
## 1 Pharma & BioTech       357 0.438  
## 2 Medical Device         225 0.276  
## 3 Non-Life Science       202 0.248  
## 4 Blood & Biologics       21 0.0257 
## 5 <NA>                     6 0.00735
## 6 Unknown                  5 0.00613

4.7 ## Summarize the ICP for the top 20 percentile for Manufacturing model profile

mx_icp.2 %>%
  count(acct_manufacturing_model, sort = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n))
## # A tibble: 19 × 3
##    acct_manufacturing_model               n     pct
##    <chr>                              <int>   <dbl>
##  1 In-House                             405 0.496  
##  2 CDMO                                 105 0.129  
##  3 CMO                                   95 0.116  
##  4 Original Equipment Manufacturing      46 0.0564 
##  5 Not Enough Info Found                 45 0.0551 
##  6 Services / Consulting                 31 0.0380 
##  7 Software / Technology Development     16 0.0196 
##  8 Consumer Packaged Goods               14 0.0172 
##  9 503B                                   9 0.0110 
## 10 Distribution / Wholesale               8 0.00980
## 11 Outsources Manufacturing               8 0.00980
## 12 CRO                                    6 0.00735
## 13 Hospital Investigational Drug Unit     6 0.00735
## 14 <NA>                                   6 0.00735
## 15 503A                                   5 0.00613
## 16 Research Institute                     5 0.00613
## 17 Testing Laboratory                     3 0.00368
## 18 CPO                                    2 0.00245
## 19 Cold Chain Warehousing                 1 0.00123

4.8 Summarize the ICP for the top 20 percentile for Manufacturing group profile

mx_icp.2 %>%
  count(manu_group, sort = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n))
## # A tibble: 2 × 3
##   manu_group            n   pct
##   <fct>             <int> <dbl>
## 1 Manufacturing       675 0.827
## 2 Non-Manufacturing   141 0.173

4.9 Summarize the ICP for the top 20 percentile for Title group (seniority) profle

mx_icp.2 %>%
  count(title_group, sort = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n))
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##   title_group            n    pct
##   <fct>              <int>  <dbl>
## 1 Manager              256 0.314 
## 2 Leadership           219 0.268 
## 3 Other                122 0.150 
## 4 Technical            120 0.147 
## 5 Missing               76 0.0931
## 6 Quality/Regulatory    23 0.0282

4.10 Summarize the ICP for the top 20 percentile for last_tactic_campaign_channel profle

mx_icp.2 %>%
  count(last_tactic_campaign_channel, sort = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n))
## # A tibble: 12 × 3
##    last_tactic_campaign_channel     n     pct
##    <chr>                        <int>   <dbl>
##  1 Online Ads                     225 0.276  
##  2 SEO                            191 0.234  
##  3 Direct/Inbound                 120 0.147  
##  4 Events                          87 0.107  
##  5 Email                           83 0.102  
##  6 Directory Listing               42 0.0515 
##  7 External Demand Gen             25 0.0306 
##  8 Outbound Prospecting            24 0.0294 
##  9 MISSING/BLANK                    9 0.0110 
## 10 Referrals                        8 0.00980
## 11 Out of Home                      1 0.00123
## 12 PR/AR/Social                     1 0.00123

4.11 Visualize the ICP segment Manufacturing Group (with % labels) for the top 20 percentile

p1 <- mx_icp.2 %>%
  count(manu_group) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = manu_group, y = pct, fill = manu_group)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(pct, accuracy = 1)),
            vjust = -0.3, size = 4) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Manufacturing Group (Percent of ICP)", x = NULL, y = "Percentage") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
p1

4.12 Visualize the ICP segment for the Title Group (with % labels) for the top 20 percentile

p2 <- mx_icp.2 %>%
  count(title_group) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = title_group, y = pct, fill = title_group)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(pct, accuracy = 1)),
            vjust = -0.3, size = 4) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Title Group (Percent of ICP)", x = NULL, y = "Percentage") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none")
p2

## Visualize the ICP segment for the Industry group (with % labels) for the top 20 percentile

p3 <- mx_icp.2 %>%
  count(acct_target_industry) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = acct_target_industry, y = pct, fill = acct_target_industry)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(pct, accuracy = 1)),
            vjust = -0.3, size = 4) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Industry (Percent of ICP)", x = NULL, y = "Percentage") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none")
p3

4.13 R Code: Conversion Rates + Sample Sizes for the Three ICP Segments-1

library(dplyr)

mx_icp_segments <- mx_icp.2 %>%
  filter(solution_rollup == "Mx") %>%
  mutate(
    progressed_num = case_when(
      progressed %in% c("yes", "Yes", "YES", 1, "1") ~ 1,
      progressed %in% c("no", "No", "NO", 0, "0") ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(
    (acct_manufacturing_model == "In-House" & title_group %in% c("Quality", "Regulatory", "Quality/Regulatory")) |
    (acct_manufacturing_model == "503A" & title_group == "Operations") |
    (acct_manufacturing_model == "503B" & title_group == "Operations")
  ) %>%
  group_by(acct_manufacturing_model, title_group) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    progressed = sum(progressed_num, na.rm = TRUE),
    conversion_rate = round(progressed / total * 100, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(conversion_rate))

mx_icp_segments
## # A tibble: 1 × 5
##   acct_manufacturing_model title_group        total progressed conversion_rate
##   <chr>                    <fct>              <int>      <dbl>           <dbl>
## 1 In-House                 Quality/Regulatory    10          6              60

4.14 R Code: Conversion Rates + Sample Sizes for the Three ICP Segments-2

library(dplyr)

quality_managers_med_device <- qal1 %>%
  filter(solution_rollup == "Mx") %>%
  mutate(
    progressed_num = case_when(
      progressed %in% c("yes", "Yes", "YES", 1, "1") ~ 1,
      progressed %in% c("no", "No", "NO", 0, "0") ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(
    acct_target_industry == "Medical Device",
    title_group %in% c("Quality", "Quality/Regulatory", "QA Manager", "Quality Manager")
  ) %>%
  group_by(acct_manufacturing_model, title_group) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    progressed = sum(progressed_num, na.rm = TRUE),
    conversion_rate = round(progressed / total * 100, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(conversion_rate))

quality_managers_med_device
## # A tibble: 5 × 5
##   acct_manufacturing_model         title_group  total progressed conversion_rate
##   <chr>                            <chr>        <int>      <dbl>           <dbl>
## 1 Not Enough Info Found            Quality/Reg…     3          2            66.7
## 2 CDMO                             Quality/Reg…     5          1            20  
## 3 In-House                         Quality/Reg…    12          2            16.7
## 4 Original Equipment Manufacturing Quality/Reg…     1          0             0  
## 5 Services / Consulting            Quality/Reg…     2          0             0

4.15 R Code: Conversion Rates + Sample Sizes for the Three ICP Segments-3

ops_managers_503 <- mx_scored %>%
  filter(solution_rollup == "Mx") %>%
  mutate(
    progressed_num = case_when(
      progressed %in% c("yes", "Yes", "YES", 1, "1") ~ 1,
      progressed %in% c("no", "No", "NO", 0, "0") ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(
    str_detect(acct_manufacturing_model, regex("503A|503B", ignore_case = TRUE)),
    str_detect(title_group, regex("Operations|Production|Ops", ignore_case = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  group_by(acct_manufacturing_model, title_group) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    progressed = sum(progressed_num, na.rm = TRUE),
    conversion_rate = round(progressed / total * 100, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(conversion_rate))
ops_managers_503
## # A tibble: 0 × 5
## # ℹ 5 variables: acct_manufacturing_model <chr>, title_group <fct>,
## #   total <int>, progressed <dbl>, conversion_rate <dbl>

4.16 R Code: Conversion Rates + Sample Sizes for two ICP Segments-1

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

quality_reg_cmo <- mx_scored %>%
  filter(solution_rollup == "Mx") %>%
  
  # Convert progressed → numeric safely
  mutate(
    progressed_num = case_when(
      progressed %in% c("yes", "Yes", "YES", 1, "1") ~ 1,
      progressed %in% c("no", "No", "NO", 0, "0") ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  
  # Filter for CMO/CDMO manufacturers
  filter(
    str_detect(acct_manufacturing_model, regex("CMO|CDMO", ignore_case = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  
  # Filter for Quality/Regulatory roles
  filter(
    str_detect(title_group, regex("Quality|Regulatory|QA|RA", ignore_case = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  
  # Summarize conversion rates
  group_by(acct_manufacturing_model, title_group) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    progressed = sum(progressed_num, na.rm = TRUE),
    conversion_rate = round(progressed / total * 100, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  
  arrange(desc(conversion_rate), desc(total))

quality_reg_cmo
## # A tibble: 2 × 5
##   acct_manufacturing_model title_group        total progressed conversion_rate
##   <chr>                    <fct>              <int>      <dbl>           <dbl>
## 1 CDMO                     Quality/Regulatory    12          2            16.7
## 2 CMO                      Quality/Regulatory     5          0             0

4.17 Filter ICP by Pharma/Biotech firms

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

tech_manu_pharma_biotech <- mx_icp.2 %>%
  filter(solution_rollup == "Mx") %>%
  
  # Convert progressed → numeric safely
  mutate(
    progressed_num = case_when(
      progressed %in% c("yes", "Yes", "YES", 1, "1") ~ 1,
      progressed %in% c("no", "No", "NO", 0, "0") ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  
  # Filter for Pharma/Biotech firms
  filter(
    str_detect(acct_target_industry, regex("Pharma|Pharmaceutical|Biotech", ignore_case = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  
  # Filter for Technical / Manufacturing roles
  filter(
    str_detect(title_group, regex("Technical|Manufacturing|Production|Engineer", ignore_case = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  
  # Summarize conversion rates
  group_by(acct_target_industry,acct_manufacturing_model, title_group) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    progressed = sum(progressed_num, na.rm = TRUE),
    conversion_rate = round(progressed / total * 100, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  
  arrange(desc(conversion_rate), desc(total))

tech_manu_pharma_biotech
## # A tibble: 10 × 6
##    acct_target_industry acct_manufacturing_model    title_group total progressed
##    <chr>                <chr>                       <fct>       <int>      <dbl>
##  1 Pharma & BioTech     CMO                         Technical       2          2
##  2 Pharma & BioTech     Software / Technology Deve… Technical       2          2
##  3 Pharma & BioTech     503A                        Technical       1          1
##  4 Pharma & BioTech     503B                        Technical       1          1
##  5 Pharma & BioTech     Distribution / Wholesale    Technical       1          1
##  6 Pharma & BioTech     CDMO                        Technical       9          5
##  7 Pharma & BioTech     Services / Consulting       Technical       2          1
##  8 Pharma & BioTech     In-House                    Technical      25          9
##  9 Pharma & BioTech     Not Enough Info Found       Technical       1          0
## 10 Pharma & BioTech     Research Institute          Technical       1          0
## # ℹ 1 more variable: conversion_rate <dbl>
mx_scored <- mx_scored %>%
  arrange(desc(.pred_yes))   # or whatever your score column is

top20 <- mx_scored %>% slice(1:floor(0.2 * n()))
top50 <- mx_scored %>% slice(1:floor(0.5 * n()))

4.18 Filter ICP by Medical Device manufacturers

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

quality_managers_med_device <- mx_scored %>%
  filter(solution_rollup == "Mx") %>%
  
  # Convert progressed → numeric safely
  mutate(
    progressed_num = case_when(
      progressed %in% c("yes", "Yes", "YES", 1, "1") ~ 1,
      progressed %in% c("no", "No", "NO", 0, "0") ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  
  # Filter for Medical Device manufacturers
  filter(
    str_detect(acct_target_industry, regex("Medical Device", ignore_case = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  
  # Filter for Quality Manager roles
  filter(
    str_detect(title_group, regex("Quality|QA|Quality Manager|QA Manager|Quality/Regulatory", ignore_case = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  
  # Summarize conversion rates
  group_by(acct_manufacturing_model, title_group) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    progressed = sum(progressed_num, na.rm = TRUE),
    conversion_rate = round(progressed / total * 100, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  
  arrange(desc(conversion_rate), desc(total))

quality_managers_med_device
## # A tibble: 5 × 5
##   acct_manufacturing_model         title_group  total progressed conversion_rate
##   <chr>                            <fct>        <int>      <dbl>           <dbl>
## 1 Not Enough Info Found            Quality/Reg…     3          2            66.7
## 2 CDMO                             Quality/Reg…     5          1            20  
## 3 In-House                         Quality/Reg…    12          2            16.7
## 4 Services / Consulting            Quality/Reg…     2          0             0  
## 5 Original Equipment Manufacturing Quality/Reg…     1          0             0

4.19 Filter ICP by manufacturing model and title group

mx_icp_segments <- top20 %>%
  filter(solution_rollup == "Mx") %>%
  mutate(
    progressed_num = case_when(
      progressed %in% c("yes", "Yes", "YES", 1, "1") ~ 1,
      progressed %in% c("no", "No", "NO", 0, "0") ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(
    (acct_manufacturing_model == "In-House" &
       title_group %in% c("Quality", "Regulatory", "Quality/Regulatory")) |

    (acct_manufacturing_model == "503A" |
       title_group == "Operations") |

    (acct_manufacturing_model == "503B" |
       title_group == "Operations")
  ) %>%
  group_by(acct_manufacturing_model, title_group) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    progressed = sum(progressed_num, na.rm = TRUE),
    conversion_rate = round(progressed / total * 100, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(conversion_rate))

mx_icp_segments
## # A tibble: 8 × 5
##   acct_manufacturing_model title_group        total progressed conversion_rate
##   <chr>                    <fct>              <int>      <dbl>           <dbl>
## 1 503A                     Leadership             4          4           100  
## 2 503A                     Technical              1          1           100  
## 3 503B                     Other                  1          1           100  
## 4 503B                     Technical              1          1           100  
## 5 In-House                 Quality/Regulatory    10          6            60  
## 6 503B                     Leadership             3          1            33.3
## 7 503B                     Manager                3          1            33.3
## 8 503B                     Missing                1          0             0

4.20 Identify the top decile using the correct score column

library(dplyr)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Identify the top decile using the correct score column
# ------------------------------------------------------------

decile_cutoff <- quantile(mx_scored$.pred_yes, 0.80, na.rm = TRUE)

top_decile <- mx_scored %>%
  filter(.pred_yes >= decile_cutoff)

4.21 Identify the top decile using Top Decile Profile (Industry + Title Group)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Top Decile Profile (Industry + Title Group)
# ------------------------------------------------------------

top_decile_industry <- top_decile %>%
  count(acct_target_industry, sort = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(share = n / sum(n))

top_decile_title <- top_decile %>%
  count(title_group, sort = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(share = n / sum(n))
top_decile_industry
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##   acct_target_industry     n   share
##   <chr>                <int>   <dbl>
## 1 Pharma & BioTech       358 0.438  
## 2 Medical Device         225 0.275  
## 3 Non-Life Science       202 0.247  
## 4 Blood & Biologics       21 0.0257 
## 5 <NA>                     6 0.00734
## 6 Unknown                  5 0.00612

Plot top decile using Top Decile Profile (Industry + Title Group)

top_decile_title %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%   # top 20 title groups
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(title_group, share),
             y = share)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#1B9E77") +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(share, accuracy = 0.1)),
            hjust = -0.1,
            size = 4) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent,
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
  labs(
    title = "Top Decile — Title Group Share (Top 20)",
    x = "Title Group",
    y = "Share of Top Decile"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

## Plot top decile industry share

library(ggplot2)

top_decile_industry %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%   # top 20 industries
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(acct_target_industry, share),
             y = share)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2C7BB6") +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(share, accuracy = 0.1)),
            hjust = -0.1,
            size = 4) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent,
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
  labs(
    title = "Top Decile — Industry Share (Top 20)",
    x = "Industry",
    y = "Share of Top Decile"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
